behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because this failure is in template-lint logic (detecting copied phase text), which can be exercised as pure input/output without module handoff. Unit then beats System because no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is required. Rejection gate: Integration/System are disqualified by behavior text "static prompt-template lint check" (single lint rule evaluation, no real infra).
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with template strings that include and omit phase-specific SKILL.md instruction fragments; assert reject/accept decisions and precise violation spans.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because leakage can be introduced at module boundaries during interpolation/fixture assembly, not only in one function. Integration beats System because this can run in-process with local fixture wiring, no real network/subprocess needed. Rejection gate: System disqualified by behavior text focused on "fully assembled prompt ... after interpolation and fixtures" rather than OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-assembly pipeline with representative fixtures and interpolation variables, then assert final prompt text has no prohibited phase-instruction substrings/patterns.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because failure appears when invocation wiring, skill-file loader, and prompt builder interact (source-of-truth handoff). Integration beats System because local file fixtures are sufficient; no real external infra boundary is required. Integration brake check: no required real AI-output judgment is stated here, only source/wiring correctness. Rejection gate: Agentic disqualified by text emphasizing "loads skill context from plugin/skill files" and "rather than hard-coded prompt rules" (provenance/wiring test).
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation end-to-end with a controlled plugin/skill directory and assert loaded context originates from file content (not embedded constants), e.g., by mutating file content and observing corresponding invocation payload changes.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this is harness-level configuration and file-variant orchestration across modules. Integration beats System because temp/local filesystem variants are enough; no real network/subprocess boundary is intrinsic. Rejection gate: System disqualified by behavior text about "same user-style prompt" and SKILL variant switching, which is local harness wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run two harness invocations (baseline skill vs degraded/missing SKILL.md) with identical test prompt text and assert only skill-context inputs differ while prompt text fixture remains byte-identical.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration initially plausible for harness wiring, but System vs Agentic floor applies because correctness depends on real model behavior changing when skill guidance is degraded. Agentic beats System since the risk is model decision/output quality, not OS/network mechanics. Agentic vs Workflow: Agentic wins because behavior describes one behavioral outcome under one prompt, not multiple model decision stages. Rejection gate: Workflow disqualified by absence of multi-step agent pipeline language.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live model test with unchanged user-style prompt against baseline and degraded skill variants; assert degraded variant violates structural behavioral expectation and test fails accordingly.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this is an ordering contract across response-capture, persistence, and parser modules. Integration beats System because local artifact writing and in-process sequencing are sufficient; no external OS primitive beyond normal local file I/O is required. Rejection gate: Agentic disqualified because behavior checks capture order, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use harness path that receives model text (stub or recorded response), assert artifact write occurs with raw payload before parser invocation (via call-order hooks/timestamps) and artifact preserves unmodified output.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because this behavior is assertion-rule logic (structure/schema validation vs keyword check) that can be validated in a single assertion component. Unit beats System because no real infra boundary is needed. Rejection gate: Integration disqualified by behavior text centered on assertion semantics, not module orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion helpers with crafted outputs: cases that contain expected keywords but wrong schema/field placement must fail; correctly structured outputs must pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration is insufficient because deterministic fixtures can hide real model-produced malformed structures. System vs Agentic floor: Agentic wins since the failure depends on real model output variability/quality under live execution. Agentic vs Workflow: Agentic wins because this is a single live-response evaluation, not a multi-agentic sequence. Rejection gate: Workflow disqualified by behavior text lacking chained model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral smoke tests where a known regression pattern can still contain expected keywords; verify structural assertions fail while an equivalent keyword-only checker would pass.
